full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Some aspects of Ash Wednesday at St. John's have long been a mystery to me.<|10.68|> <|10.68|> Not the ritual of imposing ashes or the haunting liturgy, but rather those who feel compelled<|19.58|> <|19.58|> to come.<|21.72|> <|21.72|> You all.<|22.72|> <|22.72|> Why do you come?<|25.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> You who come on Sundays and you who come just on this day.<|7.00|> <|7.00|> For years, Richard, Broderick and I have marveled at the young folks who <|25.00|> Mm-hmm.<|25.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Ash Wednesday is often called the most honest day of the year in church.<|8.22|> <|9.06|> Whether you believe all that we <|9.00|> while medicating and exercising to slim down our bodies.<|14.00|> <|14.00|> Looking younger is a multi-billion dollar business in America.<|22.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> So it is wild to want to come to a place that is going to look you in the eyes and say,<|7.62|> <|7.62|> "You are dust."<|10.06|> <|10.06|> You might be really good-looking dust, but you are dust.<|18.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We live fragile lives in what feels like an increasingly fragile world.<|9.30|> <|9.30|> Ash Wednesday is a day of truth and a day of primal connection.<|18.00|> <|18.00|> We are smeared with the ashes of burned palms,<|23.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> not the ashes He had a point. So we are reminded when we are smeared with ashes of palm<|8.46|> <|8.46|> that we are and will always be a part of God's creation.<|15.94|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We say it really counter-cultural thing of coming here today,<|19.28|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> to realize and face the truth of our mortality.<|6.24|> <|6.24|> What then does that wonder if you might feel the same way.<|24.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> If I know that this body is going to perish, how then shall I spend my days?<|11.56|> <|11.56|> If I to me, is Lent.<|2.68|> <|3.78|> It's a season of preparing to live.<|7.06|> <|7.90|> To live with a renewed sense of purpose and passion.<|12.12|> <|13.02|> A season to look closely at my live as I hope to live.<|23.86|> <|25.38|> The ways I have fallen short.<|27.38|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> As the prophet Joel admonishes, "Please God, rend my heart and not my clothing."<|11.76|> <|11.76|> While the season of Lent of preparing to live has many aspects that are deeply personal and<|17.96|> <|17.96|> introspective, isn't it fascinating that we come together as a community to embark on<|27.16|> <|27.16|> the journey?<|28.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> When you're going to kneel together, confess together, pray together, be smeared with ashes of palm together,<|11.58|> <|12.18|> there is a profound life force in the communal.<|17.94|> <|18.66|> There is great power in gathering.<|21.66|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And in our Old Testament lesson, the prophet Joel called God's people together.<|6.88|> <|7.92|> He's calling them because there's a plague of locusts in the land, and he knows they need to gather together to pray.<|16.74|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Joel calls for people to return to God, to call a solemn assembly, to gather the<|7.14|> <|7.14|> people, to sanctify the congregation, assemble the aged, gather the children,<|12.82|> <|12.82|> even infants at the breast.<|16.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> My friends, there is power in community, in what feels impossible, and so much feels impossible right now. Amen?<|13.24|> <|14.78|> What feels impossible alone can be accomplished together.<|21.16|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> There is power in gathering on this day to acknowledge where we have fallen short individually and communally.<|11.00|> <|12.00|> There is power in remembering that we are mortal, and only the triune God is immortal.<|21.34|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And there is power in deciding for ourselves how it is that we want to live out the days we have.<|7.90|> <|8.68|> How to love each other and God better.<|12.12|> <|13.22|> How to participate in the inbreaking of God's kingdom here and now and to right the wrongs of our world.<|22.68|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> My friends, if you agree with me and many others that today is the most honest day of<|9.04|> <|9.04|> the year facing our mortality, I wonder how will you spend your next 40 days?<|22.06|> <|22.06|> I wonder how you will choose to live.<|27.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Amen.<|1.08|><|endoftext|>
